tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, significant proteinuria, and glucosuria with low urine specific gravity, suggesting impaired renal function, possibly nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy, though HbA1c is unexpectedly low. Urine is cloudy, brown, and ammoniacal, but no evidence of infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Further nephrological evaluation and correlation with clinical findings are recommended.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
